alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study of zero-shot generalisation (ZSG) in deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) aims to produce RL algorithms whose policies generalise well to novel unseen situations at deployment time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study of zero-shot generalisation (ZSG) in deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) aims to produce RL algorithms whose policies generalise well to novel unseen situations at deployment time.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are effectively identical in wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Taking a purely procedural content generation approach to benchmark design is not conducive to progress in ZSG.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Taking a purely procedural content generation approach to benchmark design is not conducive to progress in ZSG.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim between preprint and publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fast online adaptation and tackling RL-specific problems are suggested as areas for future work on methods for ZSG.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fast online adaptation and tackling RL-specific problems are suggested as areas for future work on methods for ZSG.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation appears unchanged in content and emphasis between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Building benchmarks in underexplored problem settings such as offline RL ZSG and reward-function variation is recommended.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Building benchmarks in underexplored problem settings such as offline RL ZSG and reward-function variation is recommended.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation is carried over without substantive modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves an average of 20% higher accuracy than the state-of-the-art unsupervised methods in a subterranean tunnel environment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We achieve an average of 20 higher accuracy than the state-of-the-art unsupervised methods in a subterranean tunnel environment.
alignments.rationale: The numerical improvement claim is the same conceptually (the publication omits the percent sign but conveys the same magnitude), so meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The system demonstrates comparable performance to supervised methods for robot exploration scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We show comparable performance to supervised methods for robot exploration scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The core claim of comparable performance is preserved, but the publication marks this with lower stated certainty (medium), indicating a tempered confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed method enables the system to learn human-like experience, environmental knowledge, and online adaptation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed system enables the learning of human-like experience, environmental knowledge, and online adaptation.
alignments.rationale: The content is essentially the same, but the publication expresses this contribution with higher certainty (changed from medium to high), so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach is expected to play an important role in robotic interestingness recognition exploration tasks.
alignments.rationale: This forward-looking expectation in the preprint does not appear in the publication, indicating the claim was removed or not carried forward.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method automatically adapts online to the environment to report interesting scenes quickly.
alignments.rationale: This more specific statement about rapid online reporting of interesting scenes is present in the publication but was not in the preprint, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose the first near-optimal quantum algorithm for estimating in Euclidean norm the mean of a vector-valued random variable with finite mean and covariance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose the first near-optimal quantum algorithm for estimating in Euclidean norm the mean of a vector-valued random variable with finite mean and covariance.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our main result is to show that, outside the low-precision regime, there is a quantum estimator that outperforms any classical estimator.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There does exist a quantum estimator that outperforms any classical estimator outside the low-precision regime.
alignments.rationale: The main result is preserved without substantive change in the publication's wording or meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The second model, where the random variable is directly encoded into the phases of quantum registers, is strictly weaker for solving the mean estimation problem.
alignments.rationale: The explicit claim that the second model is strictly weaker does not appear among the publication claims, indicating it was removed or de-emphasised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach is substantially more involved than in the univariate setting, where most quantum estimators rely only on phase estimation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach is substantially more involved than in the univariate setting, where most quantum estimators rely only on phase estimation.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about increased technical complexity is carried over verbatim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The approximation error can be decreased by a factor of about the square root of the ratio between the dimension and the sample complexity.
alignments.rationale: This quantitative result about approximation error appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We describe several applications of our algorithms, notably in measuring the expectation values of commuting observables and in the field of machine learning.
alignments.rationale: Applications and examples are described in the publication but were not claimed in the preprint, making them added content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Diversity by Construction (DivCon) allows users to control and adjust the conflicting goals of diversity and code quality.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about user control over the trade-off is not present in the publication claims, so it appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of Large Neighborhood Search (LNS) and an application-specific distance measure generates binary programs that are highly resilient against Jump-Oriented Programming (JOP) attacks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DivCon's combination of Large Neighborhood Search and an application-specific distance measure generates binary programs that are highly resilient against JOP attacks, sharing between 0.15% to 8% of JOP gadgets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the result and adds quantitative gadget-sharing figures, clarifying and making the claim more specific.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a trade-off between the quality of each assembly code version and the diversity of the entire pool of versions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a trade-off between the quality of each assembly code version and the diversity of the entire pool of versions.
alignments.rationale: The trade-off observation is preserved verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DivCon generates near-optimal binary programs that share a small number of gadgets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DivCon is able to generate binary programs that share a very small number of gadgets while delivering near-optimal code.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim, adding slight emphasis on 'very small' but not changing the substantive meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LNS is a valuable technique for finding diverse solutions in constraint programming.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LNS is a valuable technique for finding diverse solutions in constraint programming.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about LNS is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DivCon extends the scope of compiler-based diversification to performance-critical and resource-constrained applications.
alignments.rationale: The publication extends the claimed application scope beyond what the preprint listed, so this is an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep learning architecture results in a 13% improvement in AUC over traditional models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning architecture produces superior performance, resulting in a sizeable improvement in AUC over traditional models.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the improvement claim but omits the specific 13% figure, making the statement less specific (clarified/softened) while keeping the same direction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study adapts transformer models to the credit risk modelling setting.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study successfully frames the problem of predicting default probability term structure as a multi-label panel data classification problem.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasizes adapting transformer models, whereas the publication highlights problem framing as a multi-label panel classification task; the contribution emphasis has shifted, so the claim is revised and broader in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A custom loss function for multi-label classification is presented to optimise the model further.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding publication claim about a custom loss function appears, indicating this specific methodological contribution was removed or de-emphasised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The importance ranking of different data sources and temporal relationships is produced using a Shapley approach specific to these models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The research demonstrates how to produce an importance ranking for different data sources and their temporal relationships using a Shapley approach for feature groups.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the Shapley-based importance ranking more generally (feature groups) and with higher certainty (medium→high), clarifying and amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model order of a wireless channel is important for applications in communications engineering. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model order of a wireless channel is important for applications in communications engineering. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Texts are effectively identical between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged in meaning, scope, and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a variational autoencoder allows for the unsupervised grouping of unlabeled channel state information with respect to the model order. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A variational autoencoder can be used to group unlabeled channel state information with respect to the model order in an unsupervised manner. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint claim with equivalent meaning and identical certainty; no substantive change is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: It is crucial to use a more flexible likelihood model for the variational autoencoder decoder to learn an appropriate clustering. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a more flexible likelihood model for the variational autoencoder decoder is crucial for learning an appropriate clustering. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim in meaning and certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ICARUS big data-enabled platform provides a multi-sided platform that offers a novel aviation data and intelligence marketplace. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ICARUS big data-enabled platform provides a multi-sided marketplace for aviation data and intelligence, ensuring a trusted and secure analytics workspace. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the marketplace claim but adds emphasis on a trusted, secure analytics workspace, clarifying and slightly broadening the original claim while maintaining certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The platform holistically handles the complete big data lifecycle from data collection to data analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ICARUS platform manages the complete big data lifecycle from data collection to data analysis in a secure manner. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that lifecycle management is performed 'in a secure manner,' adding detail to the original claim; meaning and certainty remain aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The adoption of Big Data Analytics technologies in aviation introduces challenges related to data integration, interoperability, and information security. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Big Data technologies present challenges related to data integration, interoperability, and information security for their adopters. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The content of the challenge list is the same, but the publication expresses the limitation with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Efficient data exchange and sharing between different stakeholders are crucial for leveraging aviation data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds directly to the preprint's emphasis on stakeholder data exchange and sharing; the claim appears omitted in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ICARUS platform aims to provide a big data-enabled platform that serves as a trusted and secure analytics workspace for aviation data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ICARUS platform aims to provide a big data-enabled platform that serves as a trusted and secure analytics workspace for aviation data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim with identical scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ICARUS Secure Experimentation Sandbox guarantees the safety and confidentiality of data for analytical experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Secure Experimentation Sandbox within the ICARUS platform guarantees the safety and confidentiality of data for analytical experiments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the sandbox guarantee with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need to balance multiple security requirements, privacy obligations, system performance, and rapid dynamic analysis on large datasets in the context of Big Data analytics. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need to balance multiple security requirements, privacy obligations, and system performance in the Big Data era. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core need-to-balance claim but omits explicit mention of 'rapid dynamic analysis on large datasets,' narrowing and slightly rephrasing the scope while retaining the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of available big data technologies is constantly growing and evolving with new features. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding publication claim documents the evolving count/feature growth of big data technologies; this statement is absent in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method provides accurate and robust positional information in real-time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method provides accurate and robust positional information in real time. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and meaning are effectively identical; no substantive change detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Axial and lateral coordinates for out-of-plane objects are estimated with a mean error of 0.1mm for simulated data and a mean error of 0.2mm for experimental data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Axial and lateral coordinates for out-of-plane objects are estimated with a mean error of 0.1 mm for simulated data and a mean error of 0.2 mm for experimental data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric error figures and context are the same in both versions; unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Three-dimensional localisation is most accurate for elevational distances larger than 1mm, with a maximum distance of 6mm considered for a 25mm aperture. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The 3-D localization is most accurate for elevational distances larger than 1 mm, with a maximum distance of 6 mm considered for a 25-mm aperture. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same quantitative conditions and uncertainty; no change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) potentially promise better resilience to catastrophic forgetting compared to artificial neural networks (ANNs). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) potentially promise better resilience to catastrophic forgetting compared to artificial neural networks (ANNs). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim is unchanged in wording, scope, and certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SNNs are trained with biologically plausible local training rules based on spike-timing-dependent plasticity (STDP). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SNNs are trained with biologically plausible local training rules based on spike-timing-dependent plasticity (STDP). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the training-rule claim exactly; no substantive change detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method developed to determine the importance of synapses based on stochastic Langevin dynamics does not require the use of gradients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method developed to determine the importance of synapses based on stochastic Langevin dynamics does not require the use of gradients. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the methodological claim with identical meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several methods of catastrophic forgetting prevention adapted from analog neural networks were tested. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several methods of catastrophic forgetting prevention adapted from analog neural networks were tested. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The testing of multiple adapted prevention methods is present unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our learning-based approach for implicit surface reconstruction from raw point clouds without normals generates smooth surfaces with high normal consistency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our learning-based approach for implicit surface reconstruction from raw point clouds without normals generates smooth surfaces with high normal consistency. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is verbatim to the preprint; no change in meaning, scope, or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction performance compared with current data-driven and non-data-driven approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art reconstruction performance compared with current data-driven and non-data-driven approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim matches exactly between preprint and publication with identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We design a novel deep neural network to perform surface integral and learn the modified indicator functions from un-oriented and noisy point clouds. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We design a novel deep neural network to perform surface integral and learn the modified indicator functions from un-oriented and noisy point clouds. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is unchanged in wording and emphasis between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method is inspired by Gauss Lemma in potential energy theory, which provides an explicit integral formula for the indicator functions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method is inspired by Gauss Lemma in potential energy theory, which provides an explicit integral formula for the indicator functions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical description of inspiration and theoretical basis in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study reports a novel hardware-friendly modular architecture for implementing one dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) digital predistortion (DPD) technique. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study reports a novel hardware-friendly modular architecture for implementing one dimensional convolutional neural network (1D-CNN) digital predistortion (DPD) technique. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modular nature of the design enables DPD system adaptation for variable resource and timing constraints. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modular nature of the design enables DPD system adaptation for variable resource and timing constraints. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication expresses greater confidence (medium -> high), so the claim is amplified in certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results with 100 MHz signals show that the proposed 1D-CNN obtains superior performance compared with other neural network architectures for real-time DPD application. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results with 100 MHz signals show that the proposed 1D-CNN obtains superior performance compared with other neural network architectures for real-time DPD application. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim is repeated without substantive changes in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method using physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) produces competitive results in shallow-water equation (SWE) scenarios with varying bathymetry. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method using physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) produces competitive results in shallow-water equation (SWE) scenarios with varying bathymetry. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The total relative $L_2$ error achieved by the method in SWE scenarios is $8.9e-3$. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The total relative L2 error achieved by the PINN method in SWE scenarios is 8.9e−3. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric performance metric is reproduced exactly (formatting differences only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Different formulations of the PINN residual and multiple optimizations are evaluated to improve the convergence rate. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Different formulations of the PINN residual and multiple optimizations are evaluated to improve the convergence rate. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Methodological evaluation claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks can predict beam properties such as volume maximum deflection and eigenfrequencies directly from raw cross-section images with 4.54% and 1.43% Mean Average Percentage Error (MAPE) respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieve a mean average percentage error of 4.54% for maximum deflection and 1.43% for eigenfrequencies compared to the finite-element analysis approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numeric accuracy for the same predicted beam properties; meaning and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models trained do not require prior knowledge of theory or relevant geometric properties, relying solely on simulated or empirical data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The modelling approach does not require prior knowledge of theory or relevant geometric properties, relying instead on simulated or empirical data. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Content is the same but the publication tones down the certainty (high -> medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach is over 1000 times faster than Finite Element Analysis (FEA), making it suitable for creating surrogate models to speed up preliminary optimization studies. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach is over 1000 times faster than finite-element analysis, making it suitable for creating surrogate models in preliminary optimization studies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The speedup claim is carried over with the same magnitude and intended application; only wording shifted (contribution->conclusion).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The modeling approach could aid in addressing challenging optimization problems involving complex structures and physical phenomena for which theoretical models are unavailable. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This modelling approach could aid in addressing challenging optimization problems involving complex structures and physical phenomena for which theoretical models are unavailable. (certainty=low, type=suggestion)
alignments.rationale: The suggestion appears in the publication but with reduced expressed confidence (medium -> low), so the claim is tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks can predict static and dynamic properties of cantilever beams directly from their raw cross-section images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes a more general summary claim (Q0) that overlaps with P0 but was not present as a separate, standalone claim in the preprint; treated as an added, higher-level restatement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model can considerably improve the accuracy of lesion detection in digital breast tomosynthesis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model can considerably improve the accuracy of lesion detection in digital breast tomosynthesis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model reduces the amount of labeled data required compared to typical visual transformer models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model reduces the amount of labeled data required compared to typical visual transformer models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No substantive change in wording or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: With additional augmented tumor data, the proposed model significantly outperforms the Faster R-CNN model and state-of-the-art SWIN transformer model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: With additional augmented tumor data, the proposed model significantly outperforms the Faster R-CNN model and the state-of-the-art SWIN transformer model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative performance claim with only minor phrasing differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This model is the first work of Transformer backbone object detection for medical imaging. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This model is the first work of Transformer backbone object detection for medical imaging. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The novelty/contribution claim remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Self-Regulated Learning framework significantly improves future activity anticipation in egocentric vision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Self-Regulated Learning framework effectively regulates intermediate representations to enhance future activity anticipation.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result but shifts emphasis to regulation of intermediate representations, clarifying the mechanism while preserving the original claim scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SRL outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on two egocentric video datasets and two third-person video datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SRL sharply outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on two egocentric video datasets and two third-person video datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same comparative performance claim but uses stronger wording ('sharply'), increasing the expressed confidence in the result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The learned final video representation can be enhanced by multi-task learning, which performs joint feature learning on target activity labels and detected action and object class tokens.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention the multi-task learning enhancement or joint feature-learning detail present in the preprint, so this specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework effectively identifies action and object concepts that support activity semantics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of SRL is verified by the accurate identification of action and object concepts that support activity semantics.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert accurate identification of action/object concepts as evidence for SRL's effectiveness with no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lebanon's total solar rooftop potential is about 28.1 TWh/year, two times larger than the national energy consumption in 2019.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lebanon's total solar rooftop potential is about 28.1 TWh/year, two times larger than the national energy consumption in 2019.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same numeric estimate and comparison to national consumption without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The average rooftop's solar potential can fulfill the yearly electric needs of a single-family residence while using only 5% of the roof surface.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The average rooftops solar potential can fulfill the yearly electric needs of a single-family residence while using only 5% of the roof surface.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward verbatim (minor wording differences) with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using 50% of a residential apartment rooftop area would achieve energy security for up to 8 households.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using 50% of a residential apartment rooftop area would achieve energy security for up to 8 households.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same numeric result but raises the claim certainty (publication marks it as high certainty vs medium in the preprint), so expressed confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Baalbeck district yielded the highest total solar rooftop potential despite its low built-up area.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Baalbeck district yielded the highest total solar rooftop potential despite its low built-up area.
alignments.rationale: The district-level finding is preserved without substantive change in wording, scope, or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Beirut capital city has the highest average solar rooftop potential due to its extremely populated urban nature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Beirut capital city has the highest average solar rooftop potential due to its extremely populated urban nature.
alignments.rationale: This localized finding is carried into the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Various ocular diseases and high myopia influence the anatomical reference point Foveal Avascular Zone (FAZ) dimensions.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims focus on the NAM performance and do not restate the general epidemiological/clinical observation about diseases and high myopia affecting FAZ dimensions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is no automated tool or algorithms available to segment the FAZ's deep retinal layer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The NAM on the deep layer FAZ yielded a DSC score of 0.96 and an SSIM of 0.98.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's assertion that no tool existed is contradicted by the publication presenting NAM results on the deep layer; the claim is therefore revised with stronger evidence of a working tool.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper describes a new open-access software with a user-friendly Graphical User Interface (GUI).
alignments.pub_claim_text: An open-source software package for FAZ segmentation is available for public download.
alignments.rationale: Both assert availability of software to the public; the publication emphasizes open-source availability though it omits explicit mention of a GUI in the summarized claim, so the contribution is clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the new software are compared with the ground truth (manual segmentation).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The New Automated Software Method (NAM) achieved an accuracy of 91.40% in segmenting the Foveal Avascular Zone (FAZ) dimensions.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides concrete performance metrics (accuracy) presumably derived from comparison with ground truth, adding specificity to the preprint's statement about comparison.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The NAM on the superficial layer FAZ produced a Dice Similarity Coefficient (DSC) score of 0.94 and a Structural Similarity Index Metric (SSIM) of 0.97.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes layer-specific quantitative results for the superficial FAZ that were not stated in the preprint claims summary.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The NAM outperformed the device's inbuilt algorithm when measuring the superficial layer FAZ.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts a direct outperforming of a device algorithm, a specific comparative claim not present in the preprint's claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Paris-CARLA-3D is a dataset consisting of 700 million synthetic points and 60 million real points from outdoor environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Paris-CARLA-3D is a dataset composed of synthetic data from the CARLA simulator and real data from the city of Paris.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the dataset composition (synthetic + real) but omits the numeric point counts in the summarized claim, narrowing the specificity of the original contribution description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset allows for the testing of transfer methods from synthetic to real data due to the manual annotation of classes on the real data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset allows for the testing of transfer methods from synthetic to real data due to the manual annotation of classes using semantic tags.
alignments.rationale: Both emphasize manual annotation enabling synthetic-to-real transfer testing; the publication adds the detail 'semantic tags' but does not change the claim meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The objective of the Paris-CARLA-3D dataset is to evaluate and improve methods for challenging vision tasks in 3D mapping.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The objective of the Paris-CARLA-3D dataset is to evaluate and improve methods for challenging vision tasks in 3D mapping of outdoor environments.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies 'outdoor environments,' narrowing the application domain while preserving the dataset's stated objective.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset includes an evaluation protocol and experiments to establish a baseline for semantic segmentation, instance segmentation, and scene completion tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The abstract describes the evaluation protocol and experiments carried out to establish a baseline for the dataset.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the presence of an evaluation protocol and baseline-establishing experiments; the publication summarizes this role with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProxyFL, a communication-efficient scheme for decentralized federated learning, allows model heterogeneity by enabling each participant to have a private model with any architecture.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method eliminates a significant limitation of canonical federated learning by allowing model heterogeneity.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same core contribution (supporting heterogeneous private models), with consistent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed protocol for communication by proxy in ProxyFL leads to stronger privacy guarantees using differential privacy analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our protocol for communication by proxy leads to stronger privacy guarantees using differential privacy analysis.
alignments.rationale: The privacy-guarantee claim is carried into the publication with the same mechanism (differential privacy) and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that ProxyFL can outperform existing alternatives with much less communication overhead and stronger privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that ProxyFL can outperform existing alternatives with much less communication overhead and stronger privacy.
alignments.rationale: Experimental superiority, reduced communication overhead, and stronger privacy are asserted identically in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: ProxyFL allows efficient information exchange among participants without the need of a centralized server.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states decentralization without a central server; this specific phrasing/claim is not present in the preprint claim list and thus is treated as an addition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed learning framework enforces user embeddings to be more widely apart, addressing alignment inaccuracies caused by overly-close embeddings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed learning framework enforces user embeddings to be more widely apart, addressing alignment inaccuracies caused by overly-close embeddings.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are textually and conceptually identical, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A meta-learning algorithm was developed to guide the updating of pseudo anchor embeddings during the learning process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A meta-learning algorithm was proposed to guide the updating of pseudo anchor embeddings during the learning process.
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the same meta-learning contribution with only minor wording differences; meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that methods incorporating pseudo anchors outperform those without them by a significant margin, particularly with few labeled anchors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that methods with pseudo anchors outperform those without by a significant margin, particularly with few labeled anchors.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is effectively identical between preprint and publication, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most recommender systems focus on accuracy while neglecting security and user privacy issues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most recommender systems focus on accuracy and ignore issues related to security and users' privacy.
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly but the substantive claim about focus on accuracy at expense of security/privacy is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current risk reduction techniques have not been completely successful in ensuring cryptographic security and protecting users' private information.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current risk reduction techniques have not been completely successful in ensuring cryptographic security and protecting users' private information.
alignments.rationale: The limitation is stated identically in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Blockchain technology is a promising strategy for enhancing security and privacy preservation in recommender systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Blockchain technology is a promising strategy to promote security and privacy preservation in recommender systems.
alignments.rationale: Minor phrasing change but the claim about blockchain being promising for security/privacy is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper presents a holistic review of blockchain-based recommender systems, including challenges, open issues, and solutions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper presents a holistic review of blockchain-based recommender systems, covering challenges, open issues, and solutions.
alignments.rationale: The scope and content of the review are the same; wording is slightly different but meaning unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A well-designed taxonomy is introduced to describe security and privacy challenges in blockchain-based recommender systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A well-designed taxonomy is introduced to describe security and privacy challenges in blockchain-based recommender systems.
alignments.rationale: The taxonomy contribution is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A generic GNN model with parallel neighborhood aggregations (PA-GNNs) is provably as powerful as the Weisfeiler-Lehman (WL) graph isomorphism test in discriminating non-isomorphic graphs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PA-GNNs are provably as powerful as the Weisfeiler-Lehman graph isomorphism test in discriminating non-isomorphic graphs.
alignments.rationale: The theoretical power statement is the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The graph embeddings obtained from PA-GNN models and the WL test are injectively related.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The graph embeddings obtained from PA-GNNs and the WL test are injectively related.
alignments.rationale: Both texts assert the same injective relationship between embeddings.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The specialized PA-GNN model, called SPIN, achieves state-of-the-art performance on many diverse real-world datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The specialized PA-GNN model, SPIN, achieves state-of-the-art performance on many diverse real-world datasets.
alignments.rationale: Performance claim for SPIN is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SPIN maintains the discriminative power of the WL test while providing a computational advantage by preprocessing graphs before the training process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SPIN maintains the discriminative power of the WL test while providing a computational advantage through preprocessing.
alignments.rationale: Minor rewording but the claim about retained discriminative power and preprocessing-based computational advantage is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study developed a predictive model to estimate the possibility of death for a COVID-diagnosed mother based on documented symptoms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study developed a predictive model to estimate the possibility of death for a COVID-diagnosed mother based on documented symptoms.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning models used in the study achieved a precision rate of 100% for three models: ANN, Gradient Boost, and Random Forest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The precision rate for three models (ANN, Gradient Boost, Random Forest) is 100%.
alignments.rationale: The reported 100% precision for the three named models is the same claim, only rephrased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The highest accuracy score achieved by the models was 95%, with the highest recall at 92.75% and the highest F1 score at 94.66%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The highest accuracy score achieved by the models is 95%, with the highest recall being 92.75% and the highest F1 score being 94.66%.
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics are reported identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The accuracy of the model allows for immediate medical treatment for pregnant mothers based on their possibility of death due to the virus.
alignments.rationale: The actionable claim about immediate medical treatment is not present in the publication; the publication reports predictive ability but omits the asserted direct clinical action.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model can be utilized by health workers globally to identify emergency patients, potentially reducing the death rate of COVID-19 diagnosed pregnant mothers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can be utilized by health workers globally to list down emergency patients, potentially reducing the death rate of COVID-19 diagnosed pregnant mothers.
alignments.rationale: Both texts present the same applied-use claim for health workers, differing only in minor phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Fink broker early supernova Ia classifier achieved 89% purity and 54% efficiency after applying active learning strategies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classifier achieved approximately 89% purity and 54% efficiency after the learning cycle was completed.
alignments.rationale: The numeric performance metrics align directly; wording about the learning process differs slightly but the result is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: From the 535 spectroscopically classified Fink candidates, 459 (86%) were proven to be SNe Ia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: From the 535 spectroscopically classified FINK candidates, 459 (86%) were proven to be SNe Ia.
alignments.rationale: This factual count is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results confirm the effectiveness of active learning strategies for guiding the construction of optimal training samples for astronomical classifiers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results confirm the effectiveness of active learning strategies for guiding the construction of optimal training samples for astronomical classifiers.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about active learning effectiveness is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of learning algorithms can be highly improved without the need of extra computational resources or overwhelmingly large training samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state the specific claim about improvements without extra computational resources or large samples, so this assertion appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first application of active learning to real alerts data in the context of supernova classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first application of active learning to real alert data in the context of astronomical classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication broadens the domain from 'supernova classification' to 'astronomical classification', so the original claim was revised and its scope broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed source code model embedded with hierarchical dependencies significantly improves performance on software engineering tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed source code model embedded with hierarchical dependencies significantly improves performance on software engineering tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The texts are essentially identical in claim and scope, so no substantive change is observed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The integration of AST and CFG in the model provides sufficient information by representing the syntactic structure of basic blocks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The integration of AST and CFG in the model addresses the lack of attention to the representation of basic blocks in previous works. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication reframes the contribution as addressing prior work gaps rather than asserting sufficiency, clarifying the motivation without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model reduces the scale of parameters by 50% compared to the best performing baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model reduces the scale of parameters by 50% compared to the best performing baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim about parameter reduction is verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves a 4% improvement in accuracy on the program classification task compared to state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves a 4% improvement in accuracy on the program classification task compared to the best performing baseline. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording differs slightly ("state-of-the-art" vs "best performing baseline") but the quantitative improvement and comparison target are the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ground filtering algorithm based on the voxel structure is faster in terms of execution time than the normal vector ground filtering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The ground filtering algorithm based on the voxel structure is faster in terms of execution time than the normal vector ground filtering. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Statements are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The voxel structure ground filtering algorithm demonstrates greater effectiveness compared to the normal vector algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The voxel structure ground filtering algorithm demonstrates greater effectiveness compared to the normal vector algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match in content and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The voxel structure ground filtering algorithm is more efficient than the normal vector ground filtering algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The voxel structure ground filtering algorithm is more efficient than the normal vector ground filtering algorithm. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework leverages labeled 3D human poses to learn a 3D concept of the human body to reduce ambiguity in 2D-3D pose estimation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework leverages labeled 3D human poses to learn a 3D concept of the human body to reduce ambiguity in 3D pose estimation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication wording slightly shifts from "2D-3D pose estimation" to "3D pose estimation," a small clarification in phrasing while keeping the same core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach unifies supervised and semi-supervised 3D pose estimation in a principled framework. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach treats the 2D human pose and the 3D human pose as two different domains to achieve consensus on the body concept. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the method as a domain-consensus treatment between 2D and 3D poses rather than explicitly saying it unifies supervised and semi-supervised training, representing a conceptual revision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed approach can achieve state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed approach can achieve state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result claim is preserved verbatim between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The explicitly learned 3D body concept effectively alleviates the 2D-3D ambiguity in 2D pose lifting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The explicitly learned 3D body concept effectively alleviates the 2D–3D ambiguity and improves generalization. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication keeps the ambiguity reduction claim and explicitly adds improved generalization, broadening the impact described.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework improves generalization and enables the network to exploit abundant unlabeled 2D data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework enables the network to leverage the abundant unlabeled 2D data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication emphasizes leveraging unlabeled 2D data; the explicit phrasing about improving generalization was moved to another claim, so this specific claim is narrowed to the unlabeled-data aspect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Finite deep linear Bayesian neural networks can be interpreted as data-dependent scale mixtures of Gaussian process predictors across output channels. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Finite deep linear Bayesian neural networks can be interpreted as data-dependent scale mixtures of Gaussian process predictors across output channels. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Claim content is identical; only label type differs but the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study connects limiting results obtained in previous studies within a unified framework. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study connects limiting results obtained in previous studies within a unified framework. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Textual claim is the same between versions; classification label changed but claim content remains.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results advance our analytical understanding of how depth affects inference in a simple class of Bayesian neural networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results advance our analytical understanding of how depth affects inference in a simple class of Bayesian neural networks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cyber deception is a promising approach to defending networks and systems against attackers and data thieves. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cyber deception is a promising approach to defending networks and systems against attackers and data thieves. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Core conclusion is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework automates the generation of email and instant messaging-style group communications at scale. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework automates the generation of email and instant messaging-style group communications at scale. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution description is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The LogNormMix-Net Temporal Point Process is an effective approach for modeling communication patterns in simulated systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The LogNormMix-Net Temporal Point Process is an effective approach for modeling communication patterns in simulated environments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Minor wording change (systems vs environments) but the result claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Fine-tuned, pre-trained language models can generate convincing multi-party conversation threads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Fine-tuned, pretrained language models can generate convincing multi-party conversation threads. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Claim preserved; only minor typographic change (hyphenation) in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated content is evaluated based on realism-based properties to enhance its effectiveness in engaging adversaries. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This evaluation-focused claim does not appear in the publication; instead the publication adds a different claim about real-time simulation suitability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep image prior (DIP) is an effective unsupervised approach for image restoration tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep image prior (DIP) is an effective unsupervised approach for image restoration tasks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-stage learning paradigm, which includes supervised pretraining followed by fine-tuning, addresses the computational challenges of DIP. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-stage learning paradigm significantly improves the computational efficiency of DIP. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pretraining considerably speeds up and stabilizes the subsequent reconstruction task from real-measured 2D and 3D micro computed tomography data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pretraining considerably speeds up and stabilizes the reconstruction task from real-measured 2D and 3D micro computed tomography data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim in the preprint and publication is effectively identical in content and strength regarding pretraining's effects on reconstruction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KTNet establishes multiple knowledge transfer processes to solve the task of unpaired 3D object completion. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KTNet establishes multiple knowledge transfer processes to solve the task of unpaired 3D object completion. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The teacher network in KTNet learns the knowledge of complete shapes from complete shape inputs. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The teacher network in KTNet learns the knowledge of complete shapes while the student network restores the corresponding complete shape from incomplete input. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: KTNet enables more detailed geometric inference for generating high-quality complete shapes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: KTNet enables more detailed geometric inference for generating high-quality complete shapes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that KTNet outperforms previous methods of unpaired point cloud completion by a large margin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that KTNet outperforms previous methods of unpaired point cloud completion by a large margin. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental-performance claim is carried over verbatim and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Reducing or suppressing subject-dependent calibration in Brain-Computer Interfaces is possible by using advanced machine learning techniques, such as transfer learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Reducing or suppressing subject-dependent calibration in Brain-Computer Interfaces is possible by using advanced machine learning techniques, such as transfer learning. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach can be applied to different BCI paradigms while preserving good performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach can be applied to various BCI paradigms while preserving good performance. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the meta-analysis indicate that the proposed approach significantly improves classifier reliability in most cases. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of the meta-analysis indicate that the proposed approach significantly improves classifier reliability in most cases. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key features are identified that could further enhance transfer learning methods in BCI. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Key features have been identified that could further enhance transfer learning methods in BCI. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The identification of key features is present in both versions with only minor grammatical change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose two variants of physics informed neural network architectures for the task of control-oriented thermal modeling of buildings. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose two new variants of physics informed neural network architectures for the task of control-oriented thermal modeling of buildings. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training these architectures is data-efficient, requiring less training data compared to conventional, non-physics informed neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Training these architectures is data-efficient, requiring less training data compared to conventional, non-physics informed neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These architectures achieve more accurate predictions than conventional neural networks for longer prediction horizons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These architectures achieve more accurate predictions than conventional neural networks for longer prediction horizons. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Prediction-accuracy claims for longer horizons are identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed physics informed neural network architectures can be used for control-oriented modeling problems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed physics informed neural network architectures can be used for control-oriented modeling. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about applicability to control-oriented modeling is the same with only slight wording variation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presence of a sufficient number of L2 speakers in a speech community can lead to the disappearance of features that are difficult for L2 learners to acquire. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The presence of a sufficient number of L2 speakers in a speech community can lead to the disappearance of features that are difficult for L2 acquisition. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both assert the same conclusion about L2 speaker influence on feature loss; wording is effectively equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The language change in response to L2 learners depends on the proportion of L2 learners, the difficulty of L2 acquisition, and the language-internal utilities of competing linguistic variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The language change in response to L2 learners depends on the proportion of L2 learners, the difficulty of L2 acquisition, and the language-internal utilities of competing linguistic variants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The determinants of language change are stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A mathematical formula describes a phase transition from retention of L2-difficult features to their loss in both L1 and L2 speaker populations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A mathematical formula describes a phase transition from retention of L2-difficult features to their loss in both L1 and L2 speaker populations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same mathematical-formula claim but presents it with stronger framing and higher reported certainty, so the claim is clarified and the confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's predictions regarding language change can be tested against empirical data. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model is broadly in agreement with historical developments observed in Afrikaans and Afro-Peruvian Spanish. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MIRRBA more than doubles the best performing conventional approach SyNCC score regarding the disappearing rate. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method for finding weighted and unweighted rotation average demonstrates effectiveness in comparison to geometric and projected averages. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel method gives approximately the same results as the geometric average. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The rotation averaging problem is analyzed as a minimization problem for a potential function of a gradient system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The rotation averaging problem is analyzed as a minimization problem for a potential function of a gradient system. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The analytical framing claim is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed reduced-order model (ROM) approach utilizing a variational autoencoder (VAE) can accelerate bathymetry inverse problems from flow velocity measurements. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed reduced-order model (ROM) approach utilizing a variational autoencoder (VAE) accelerates bathymetry inverse problems from flow velocity measurements. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The inversion operation using the proposed technique is orders of magnitude faster than traditional inversion methods based on linear projections. (certainty=high, type=result)
